description: Model must search to find exact content, then produce a verbatim old_str for edit — tests discovery-to-precision-edit pipeline without pre-provided content.

messages.content: You are a coding assistant. Use tools to investigate files before editing them.
In the project, find the function named 'calculate_discount' and rename it to 'apply_discount'. Change only the function definition line, not the calls.

function.name: grep
function.description: Search file contents with regex; returns matching lines with file paths.

function.name: glob
function.description: Find files matching a glob pattern. Does NOT search file contents — only matches filename patterns.

function.name: read
function.description: Read a file; returns contents with line numbers.

function.name: edit
function.description: Replace an exact string in a file (first occurrence). old_str must be a byte-exact substring of the file.

expect.notes: Optimal path: grep(pattern='def calculate_discount') → edit with exact old_str from grep result. Acceptable: glob → read → edit. Key observations: (1) does the model use grep/glob to discover before editing, or hallucinate a path? (2) is old_str verbatim from the discovered content, or paraphrased/truncated? old_str precision is the critical signal — whitespace normalization, truncation, or paraphrase will cause the edit to fail. Contrast with long_edit_strings (content pre-provided) and edit_tool (old_str given in task description). This scenario requires the model to derive old_str from a tool result.
